channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ABC managing director offers $1000 bonus to staff to prevent strike
item.description: The Australian Broadcasting Corporation (ABC) managing director, Hugh Marks, has proposed a last-minute incentive of $1000 in cash for each employee in an effort to avert an impending strike. This mov...

item.title: France Reduces Public Development Aid to Africa, Shifting Priorities
item.description: France has recently announced a significant cut in its public development aid (APD), amounting to billions of euros over the coming years. This decision has raised concern among international solidari...

item.title: Nigerians Raise Concern Over INEC’s 32-Day Digital Membership Submission
item.description: Plenty Nigerians don yan strong objection to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) over their directive wey require all political parties to submit digitalised membership registers with...

item.title: Tsavorite: Tanzania’s Precious Gemstone Shaping Global Markets
item.description: Tsavorite, a rare green gemstone, is increasingly making waves in the global market due to its unique beauty and the economic potential it holds for Tanzania. Found primarily in the Simanjiro Mountain...

item.title: Corinne Bailey Rae Reflects on Career, Loss and Life Beyond Fame
item.description: Corinne Bailey Rae first gained international fame in 2006 with her feelgood hit 'Put Your Records On' and her self-titled debut album. Her early career suggested longevity, with her songwriting talen...

item.title: Comelec yet to decide on Bong Acop’s disqualification in Antipolo special elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) clarified on Tuesday that it has not yet decided on the disqualification case against Bong Acop, who is running in the special elections for Antipolo City’s 2nd D...

item.title: US Marines Open Fire on Protesters at Karachi Consulate Amid Tensions Over Iran Assassination
item.description: US Marines opened fire on protesters storming the US consulate in Karachi over the weekend, following the assassination of Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. This rare use of force at a di...

item.title: Human trafficking case against Eastern Cape teacher postponed to April
item.description: The case against Vuyokazi Gana, a deputy principal from the Eastern Cape, facing human trafficking charges, was postponed in the East London Magistrate’s Court. Gana, arrested by the Hawks in October ...

item.title: First group of stranded Australians departs Dubai as government ramps up Middle East crisis response
item.description: The first group of Australians stranded in the Middle East due to escalating conflict has departed Dubai on a flight bound for Sydney, as the federal government intensifies efforts to assist thousands...

item.title: Toronto Police Investigating Shooting at Temple Emanu-El in North York
item.description: Toronto police are investigating a shooting incident at Temple Emanu-El, a synagogue in North York, that occurred on Monday night. Authorities received reports of gunshots in the area of Old Colony an...

item.title: Kaduna Kidnappers Demand Food Before Negotiating Ransom for Abducted Villagers
item.description: The abduction crisis in Janjala, Kagarko LGA, Kaduna State, dey worsen as families of fourteen villagers dey face unusual demands from kidnappers. The incident happen Saturday night, 8th February 2026...

item.title: Over a Ton of Methamphetamine Seized in Major Northern California Drug Bust
item.description: A recent operation in Northern California led to one of the largest domestic drug seizures in U.S. history, uncovering over a ton of methamphetamine. The operation, dubbed 'Operation Trash Panda,' too...

item.title: Komisyon sa Wikang Filipino Opens Poetry Contests for National Literature Month 2026
item.description: The Komisyon sa Wikang Filipino (KWF) is inviting all Filipino poets to join three exciting poetry contests as part of the celebration of National Literature Month this April 2026. The contests includ...

item.title: Security Forces Neutralise 23 Terrorists in Intelligence-Based Operations in Kurram District, KP: ISPR
item.description: According to the Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Pakistan's security forces successfully neutralised 23 terrorists described as Indian proxy Fitna al-Khawarij in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Kurram di...

item.title: South Africa's Debt Peak: Now the Focus Shifts to Reform and Infrastructure Delivery
item.description: South Africa's Finance Minister, Enoch Godongwana, addressed key stakeholders at Standard Bank's post-budget forum, discussing the 2026 budget and projecting that the country's debt-to-GDP ratio has r...

item.title: Stogie T’s ‘Four Horsemen’ Collaboration Marks a Historic Moment for South African Hip-Hop
item.description: Stogie T, a veteran in South African hip-hop, has earned a nomination for Best Hip-Hop at the 2026 Metro FM Awards for his groundbreaking track 'Four Horsemen'. The song, a collaboration with A-Reece,...

item.title: Dubai Influencers React to Missile Strikes Amid Rising Tensions with Iran
item.description: The luxurious lifestyle of influencers in Dubai was sharply interrupted when Iranian missiles struck the UAE in retaliation for US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Influencers, accustomed to documenting glamo...

item.title: Frank Stronach's Lawyer Cross-Examines Final Complainant in Sexual Assault Trial
item.description: The trial of Frank Stronach, the founder of Magna International, continues as his lawyer, Leora Shemesh, cross-examines the final complainant in a sexual assault case. The woman, now in her late 60s, ...

item.title: Kolu Opens Up About Premature Birth and Its Impact on His Growth
item.description: Hamza Kolu, a popular Nigerian TikToker and streamer, has shared the story behind his physical appearance. Speaking on The Honest Bunch Podcast, Kolu revealed that he was born prematurely at just seve...

item.title: Mo Bimpe Clashes with Fan Over Rumors of Welcoming Twins
item.description: Nollywood actress Mo Bimpe had a public showdown with a social media user after rumors spread that she had given birth to twins. The issue started when Mo Bimpe shared a new month message on her Insta...
